Global Data Centres and Electricity Consumption

Data centres for artificial intelligence and other forms of computing account for a small share of worldwide electricity consumption, according to estimates prepared by the International Energy Agency (IEA) and other researchers.

Consumption is growing very rapidly but from a low base and the extra demand will be dwarfed by increases from industrial users, electric vehicles, domestic appliances and airconditioning over the next decade.

From a global perspective, increased electricity demand from data centres is likely to have only a moderate impact on prices, service and reliability for other users.

But data centres are strongly clustered in a small number of countries, and within them a small number of regions, a pattern that is expected to persist.

In these regions, data centres account for a high and growing share of consumption, having a much greater impact on other users.

Data centres and artificial intelligence are expected to pose a relatively modest challenge for the global energy system in the future.

But managing their local impacts on availability, cost and reliability for other users is already proving a significant challenge ...

EIA weekly petroleum status: US crude inventories fell by 1.7 million barrels, while a further 3 million barrels were released from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR), taking the cumulative post-war drawdown to almost 100 million barrels. Gasoline stocks declined by 1.5 million barrels, with Gulf Coast inventories falling to their lowest level since 2017, highlighting continued regional tightness during the peak summer driving season. Distillate inventories, however, rose by 4.6 million barrels, providing some welcome relief to the segment of the refined products market that has experienced the greatest supply stress in recent months. 
Elsewhere, inventories at the Cushing delivery hub climbed back above 20 million barrels. US crude exports increased to 3.7 million bpd but remained below the one-year average of 4.2 million bpd and well below the record 6.4 million bpd reached in May. 
Domestic crude production was unchanged, while four-week average implied demand for both gasoline and distillates eased, broadly in line with normal seasonal trends.

White House doesn't rule out taking action against open source AI models According to US financial media Semafor, a senior White House official said on Tuesday that the Trump administration has left room for future administrative actions to deal with issues related to open source artificial intelligence (AI) models.

The official mentioned that the government has "a lot of ongoing work" going on in addition to an executive order signed by Trump last month. The executive order establishes a voluntary review mechanism for AI models.

The order "also includes open source scanning and coordination mechanisms," National Cyber Director Sean Cairncross told reporters at a July 2 press conference to launch the mechanism. "We cannot achieve the president's vision without protecting and strengthening our open source ecosystem," he added.

His comments reflected the administration's desire to strengthen the country's nascent open source industry, which is increasingly worried about being overtaken by the competition.

Companies including Reflection AI recently proposed a new framework for open source AI models to the Trump administration, sparking speculation that the government might issue an executive order or other related guidance documents.

Morgan Stanley: All iPhone 18 series may increase in price by US$200, Apple's profits are expected to be boosted Morgan Stanley said in its latest research report that the tight supply of global memory chips has pushed up component costs, but Apple, with its strong pricing power, is expected to protect profit margins through product price increases and further increase revenue and earnings per share.

The bank maintained its "overweight" rating and target price of $360 for Apple. Morgan Stanley predicts that Apple’s increase in selling prices for each product line may drive third-quarter earnings per share 2% to 4% higher than current expectations, and drive full-year earnings per share in fiscal 2027 by about 1%.

Although Apple has not yet announced the pricing of the iPhone 18 series, the bank believes that the possibility of a unified price increase of US$200 for all models is rising. The research report pointed out that demand for Apple products is relatively insensitive to price changes.

The historical price elasticity of iPhone is only 0.2 to 0.5, which means that if the selling price increases by 10%, shipments may only decrease by 2% to 5%; the price elasticity of Mac and iPad is about 0.8 to 1.0.

After Apple raised the prices of MacBook and iPad in June this year, the delivery cycle of related products did not change significantly, indicating that the price increase has not significantly curbed demand. Cost pressure mainly comes from the memory shortage caused by the AI boom.

Morgan Stanley predicts that the average cost of DRAM purchased by Apple will increase by approximately 370% from fiscal 2025 to fiscal 2027, and the cost of NAND will increase by approximately 280% during the same period. If memory prices continue to remain high, Apple may raise prices again in fiscal year 2028.

The bank believes that Apple's brand loyalty, supply chain bargaining power, and supply pressure faced by competitors make it easier to pass costs on to consumers.

In addition to price increases, the new generation of Siri with intelligent capabilities and the foldable screen iPhone are also expected to promote the long-term AI replacement cycle and support iPhone demand until 2028. Nokia and NVIDIA launch AI-RAN commercial platform. What changes will happen to base stations in the future?

Nokia announced on its official website that the company has launched the industry's first commercial AI-RAN (artificial intelligence-radio access network) platform, which can significantly improve data transmission capabilities without increasing existing infrastructure.

Nokia wrote in a press release that the technology is based on Nokia's AI-native anyRAN software and NVIDIA's Aerial AI-RAN platform, which can achieve a leap in network performance and economic benefits. The platform will begin pilot deployment at the end of this year and will be officially commercialized in 2027.

Nokia said the move could help telecom operators carry more traffic in dense cells while reducing cost per bit and improving user experience. Nokia and Nvidia believe that the new platform will fundamentally change the way mobile networks operate.

"In the future, wireless networks will gradually move away from traditional architectures that rely on dedicated hardware and shift to a software-defined model that runs on general-purpose computing platforms." The ultimate goal of the cooperation between the two parties is to further bring data processing capabilities closer to users, thereby reducing transmission delays and allowing more NVIDIA chips to enter mobile communication networks.

U.S. Stock Futures Point Higher as Markets Digest Strong CPI and Ongoing Bank Earnings

U.S. equity futures were modestly higher early Wednesday as investors reacted to Tuesday’s better-than-expected inflation data and continued strength in bank earnings.

S&P 500 futures ($ES) rose 0.15 percent while Nasdaq 100 futures ($NQ) gained about 0.5 percent. Dow Jones Industrial Average futures ($YM) were roughly flat.

Markets had rallied on Tuesday after the June consumer price index showed a slight decline in core prices, easing some concerns about inflation. Traders now await the June producer price index at 8:30 a.m. Eastern for further clues on price pressures.

Bank Earnings Continue

Earnings season remains in focus. Morgan Stanley ($MS) is scheduled to report results, following strong performances from JPMorgan Chase & Co. ($JPM), Bank of America Corp. ($BAC), Goldman Sachs Group Inc. ($GS), and Wells Fargo & Co. ($WFC) on Tuesday. BlackRock Inc. ($BLK) is also set to release its quarterly numbers.

Notable Stock Movers

Several individual names saw sharp moves in premarket trading. PayPal Holdings Inc. ($PYPL) surged more than 18 percent after reports that Stripe and Advent International are in talks for a roughly $53 billion acquisition. ASML Holding N.V. ($ASML) rose about 4 percent after the chip equipment maker raised its guidance on strong AI demand.

On the downside, Pentair PLC ($PNR) fell more than 14 percent after lowering its revenue outlook. Lucid Group Inc. ($LCID) extended losses, while International Business Machines Corp. ($IBM) steadied after Tuesday’s sharp drop on a profit warning.

Geopolitical Update

The U.S. Central Command announced a new round of strikes against Iran early Wednesday aimed at degrading military capabilities used to target shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Markets showed limited immediate reaction to the news, with oil prices rising only modestly.

What to Watch

Investors will focus on the PPI report and Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh’s testimony later in the day. Additional Fed speakers are also scheduled. The market will continue monitoring developments in the Middle East and any further reaction to the latest strikes.

Just in: Cooling inflation boosts market sentiment, and AI transactions pick up across the board; Trump revokes strait transit fees, and the United States resumes blocking Iranian ports.

Abstract: Cooling inflation has boosted market sentiment, and AI transactions have picked up across the board; Trump has revoked strait transit fees, and the United States has resumed blocking Iranian ports; DeepSeek is said to be preparing to submit an IPO application as early as this year. U.S. stock market: The three major U.S.

stock indexes closed higher on July 14.

As of the close, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 9.63 points from the previous trading day to close at 52508.27 points, an increase of 0.02%; the S&P 500 stock index rose 28.25 points to close at 7543.59 points, an increase of 0.38%; the Nasdaq Composite Index rose 233.83 points to close at 26107.01 points, an increase of 0.9%.

Most large technology stocks rose. Nvidia and Intel rose by more than 4%, ASML and AMD rose by more than 2%, Google rose by nearly 2%, Qualcomm fell by more than 3%, and Arm fell by nearly 6%.

Storage concept stocks surged, with SK Hynix rising over 27% and closing at a 51% premium to Korean listed stocks, SanDisk rising over 5%, and Micron Technology rising nearly 5%. Cryptocurrency concept stocks, computer hardware, metals and mining sectors all rose.

Conte Technology rose by more than 9%, Dell Technology rose by more than 7%, Strategy rose by nearly 6%, Quantum and Southern Copper rose by more than 4%, American Silver Company rose by nearly 4%, Applied Materials rose by more than 3%, and Coinbase rose by more than 2%.

Medical equipment and weight loss drugs fell, with Abbott falling more than 3% and Eli Lilly falling more than 2%. IBM closed down 25%, evaporating nearly $70 billion in market value. Popular Chinese concept stocks had mixed gains and losses, with the Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index falling 0.11%.

Xpeng Group rose by more than 3%, iQiyi and Li Auto rose by more than 2%, Weilai rose by more than 1%, NetEase fell by more than 2%, and Baidu fell by more than 3%. European stock market: All three major European stock indexes rose on July 14.

The Financial Times' average price index of 100 stocks in the London stock market closed at 10529.39 points, up 31.1 points or 0.3% from the previous trading day; the CAC40 index in the Paris stock market in France closed at 836 6.85 points, an increase of 2.2 points, or 0.03%, from the previous trading day; the DAX index of the Frankfurt stock market in Germany closed at 25147.03 points, an increase of 32.78 points, or 0.13%, from the previous trading day.

Commodity market: International oil prices rose on July 14. As of the close of the day, the continuous contract of WTI crude oil futures on the New York Mercantile Exchange rose by $1.69 to close at $79.83 per barrel, an increase of 2.16%. The continuous contract of COMEX gold futures for the month rose by US$52.60, or 1.31%, to US$4,058.3 per ounce.

The continuous contract of COMEX silver futures for the month rose by US$1.07, or 1.84%, to US$59.04 per ounce. Spot gold rose $51.83, or 1.30%, to $4,052.96 per ounce. Spot silver rose $1.05, or 1.82%, to $58.71 per ounce. U.S. CPI growth fell back to 3.5% year-on-year in June, with cooling inflation boosting market sentiment Data released by the U.S.

Bureau of Labor Statistics showed that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 3.5% year-on-year in June, a decline from the sustained upward trend in previous months and lower than market expectations. The data boosted market expectations that the Federal Reserve may not need to raise interest rates further. Affected by this, the three major U.S.

stock indexes all closed higher on Tuesday. However, analysts pointed out that the inflation level of 3.5% is still significantly higher than the Fed's long-term target of 2%, and the outlook for monetary policy remains uncertain.

The latest estimates from the Social Security Administration show that the Social Security annual cost of living adjustment (COLA) in 2027 may be between 3.7% and 3.8%, reflecting that although inflation has cooled, it is still at a high level.

Warsh reiterated his commitment to the 2% inflation target, emphasized the independence of monetary policy, and did not hint at the interest rate path.

According to CNBC, Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Kevin Warsh gave his first public testimony at a congressional hearing on Tuesday, promising to promote "system changes" in monetary policy and eliminate the "hidden tax burden" caused by inflation on the American people.

Warsh said that even in the face of criticism from the president, he would "act based on the data" and emphasized that the cooling of CPI in June does not mean that the inflation problem has been solved.

According to analysis by Nick Timiraos, reporter of "New Federal Reserve News Service", Warsh demonstrated a zero-tolerance stance on high inflation during the hearing, but deliberately avoided specific hints on the future path of interest rates, which left a large space for interpretation of market expectations.

Warsh also expressed his commitment to improving the transparency and predictability of the Fed's policy framework. Trump revokes 20% transit fee in Strait of Hormuz, while US resumes blockade of Iranian ports According to CNBC, U.S. President Trump announced on Tuesday that the ceasefire between the United States and Iran has ended, and the U.S.

military immediately launched a new round of air strikes against Iran near the Strait of Hormuz. At the same time, Trump’s previously proposed plan to impose a 20% toll on the Strait of Hormuz has been withdrawn. Trump said the United States would be a "guardian" of the vital waterway - through which about 20% of the world's oil shipments pass.

Iran is trying to prevent oil tankers from passing a U.S.-designated route, opening fire on ships approaching the Omani coast. In response, U.S. Central Command launched a three-day strike against Iranian targets near the strait. "Through in-depth conversations with leaders in the Middle East, I have decided to replace the 20% U.S.

fee reimbursement mechanism with a trade and investment agreement between the Gulf countries and the United States," Trump posted on Truth Social on Tuesday. Trump said the attack on Iran will continue "until I say 'enough'." He also said that he would save the attack on Iran's energy facilities until the end.

In addition, Trump said that the United States held talks with Iran that day and the United States urged Iran to reach an agreement. So far, Iran has not responded to this. U.S. Treasury Department: Overseas U.S. bond holdings rebounded in May, reaching the second highest level in history Data released by the U.S.

Treasury Department on Tuesday showed that U.S. Treasury bonds held by overseas investors increased by $18.5 billion in May from April, reaching $9.37 trillion, the second highest level in history. U.S. Treasury holdings increased by $3.9 billion in the previous month.

Canada led the way with a US$38.7 billion increase in positions, followed by a US$11.1 billion increase in UK positions and a US$8.2 billion increase in mainland China's positions. However, Japan's positions, the largest holder, decreased by US$66.8 billion.

The market generally linked this to Japan's intervention in the foreign exchange market and the sale of US debt in exchange for funds.

DeepSeek is preparing to submit an IPO application as early as this year According to Bloomberg, Hangzhou-based DeepSeek has begun preparations for listing in the mainland and plans to submit a listing application this year so that it can be listed in 2027. One of them also said the startup is currently in talks with accounting and banking advisers.

DeepSeek has begun talks with new investors for a new funding round, aiming to increase its pre-money valuation to at least 480 billion yuan ($71 billion). Institutions participating in the investment include well-known companies such as Tencent Holdings (https://t.co/6huzlu2JwY) and CATL (https://t.co/sggtyiPzI0).

According to relevant sources, DeepSeek hopes to raise at least another 10 billion yuan in funding. However, the final financing amount may be higher, depending on the number of institutions participating in the investment.

OpenAI plans to launch its first hardware product - a home smart speaker According to reports, OpenAI is developing a home smart speaker as its first own hardware product, which is scheduled to be released next year. This product will integrate OpenAI’s large language model capabilities and aims to enter the consumer smart home market.

However, Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI may interfere with the product's release schedule. This marks an important extension of OpenAI from pure software and API services to the field of hardware terminals, and will also bring it into direct competition with existing smart speaker products such as Amazon Echo and Apple HomePod.

The United States and the United Kingdom join forces to promote regulatory cooperation on stablecoins, and the Trump administration accelerates the digital layout of the U.S.

dollar The United States and the United Kingdom have joined forces to promote a transatlantic regulatory framework for stablecoins and issued a series of policy recommendations aimed at establishing a clear path and eliminating regulatory barriers for the issuance of stablecoins in both countries.

Relevant initiatives originate from the "Transatlantic Working Group on Future Markets" established in September last year, covering cross-border tokenized asset testing, regulatory coordination and review of bank crypto asset capital treatment. U.S.

Treasury Secretary Bessent said the relevant measures reflected the two countries' "shared commitment to promoting economic growth and promoting global standards that reward innovation and competition." This cooperation is the latest move by the Trump administration in the United States to vigorously support stablecoin policies.

Trump signed the "Genius Act" in July 2025, officially establishing a regulatory framework for the development of stablecoins in the United States. Changxin Technology’s issuance price is 8.66 yuan/share Changxin Technology (https://t.co/6hQvgdswLP) announced the IPO price of the company's Science and Technology Innovation Board at 8.66 yuan per share.

Before Changxin Technology exercised its over-allotment option, the total post-issuance share capital was 66,880,886,077 shares. Based on this calculation, Changxin Technology’s market value would be 579.188 billion yuan. If successfully listed, Changxin’s market value would rank 28th in the A-share market. SK Hynix’s U.S.

stock surged 27%, with a premium of more than 45% over South Korea’s underlying stocks, and ADR options were officially listed for trading. The share price of SK Hynix (https://t.co/TWGNdA7Ftf) rose by more than 27% to US$193.92 per share, a new high since its listing, with a premium of more than 45% over South Korea's underlying stocks.

The current total market value is US$1.36 trillion. The direct trigger for the rise was the release of a report titled "Staying Greedy When Others Are Fearful" by top research institution SemiAnalysis, which was clearly bullish on SK Hynix. SK Hynix American Depositary Receipt (ADR) options officially began trading on the U.S.

Options Exchange on Tuesday, providing investors in the world's largest derivatives market with a new tool to participate in the volatile market conditions of the leading AI memory chip company.

Daniel Kirsch, head of options business at Piper Sandler, said that investors are expected to focus on short-term trading opportunities as SK Hynix ADR continues to rise this week. As of press time, SK Hynix's Korean stock price rose more than 10% in early trading.

Nvidia and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries plan to cooperate in AI data center cooling and power fields According to Nikkei, Nvidia (https://t.co/FMZWXrE0to) is considering establishing a partnership with Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (https://t.co/5BOeNdgGqN), which is expected to provide cooling systems and energy management equipment for AI data centers.

Nvidia calls its next-generation AI data center an "AI factory" and plans to build related facilities with global partners. In Japan, South Korea's SK Group is cooperating with Nvidia and plans to build an AI factory from 2028 to 2029.

Mitsubishi Heavy Industries produces air conditioning, high-efficiency cooling equipment and emergency power supply equipment required for data centers, and provides energy management systems that integrate the above technologies.

Apple evaluates AI model compression technology: 27 billion parameter large model can be run directly on iPhone, paving the way for Siri upgrade Apple (https://t.co/DVmhopYweF) is in early discussions with Silicon Valley startup PrismML, which claims to be able to compress large AI models to run natively on the iPhone.

If the technical verification is passed, this move will strengthen Apple's privacy proposition and provide key support for its long-delayed Siri upgrade. PrismML CEO said Apple has begun evaluating its technology.

The negotiations are currently at a very early stage and the direction is not yet clear, but they are "progressing smoothly." The news comes just a day after Apple launched the public beta of iOS 27 - the first widespread test open to the public after Apple's overhaul of Siri, designed to compete with AI assistants from OpenAI and Anthropic.

For Apple, keeping more AI computing locally on the device means lower response latency, less cloud computing costs, and the ability to use some functions without being connected to the Internet—all of which are highly consistent with its core privacy positioning. Lucid denies rumors of considering going private or filing for bankruptcy U.S.

electric car maker Lucid Group (https://t.co/H5RoSzSfc5) denied a report in a blog post on Tuesday local time, saying it was "completely untrue" that the company was considering a privatization transaction or filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. During the U.S.

stock market, the company's stock price once plummeted by more than 50%, the largest one-day drop since its listing. However, after the company denied rumors of privatization or bankruptcy, the stock price decline eventually narrowed to 16%.

Lucid stated in a statement that as the company's recently released quarterly documents show, the company has sufficient liquidity to support operations for a long time next year; the company's board of directors has not established a special committee to evaluate the above-mentioned rumored plan.

At the same time, corporate restructuring consultant AlixPartners is currently helping Lucid improve execution efficiency and operational performance, rather than recommending that the company file for bankruptcy.

Buffett plans to 'clear out' Berkshire Hathaway stock On Tuesday local time, the legendary American "stock god" Buffett announced that he plans to completely dispose of his remaining Berkshire Hathaway shares in the next eight years. Buffett, who will celebrate his 96th birthday next month, currently holds more than $140 billion in Berkshire shares.

According to Berkshire's announcement, Buffett converted 8,000 shares of Berkshire-A (https://t.co/ZvYY9Dmdda) into 12 million shares of Berkshire-B (https://t.co/oDM2YiN0US) on Tuesday, then donated 9 million shares to a charity named after his late first wife, Susan Thompson Buffett, and donated 1 million shares to each of his three children's foundations.

The total market value is slightly more than 5.9 billion US dollars. Anthropic launches teacher version of Claude, Stride stock price falls under pressure AI giants have entered the K-12 education market, disrupting the education technology sector.

On July 14, Anthropic announced the launch of Claude for Teachers, opening its advanced AI capabilities to K-12 in-service teachers in the United States for free, directly impacting the core business of educational technology company Stride (https://t.co/pEBpC132wK) and triggering market concerns about the latter's competitive position.

After the news was announced, Stride's stock price fell as much as 7.7% intraday, and finally closed down about 5.6% to $86.88, the largest single-day drop since June 15 this year, when the stock fell 14.5% due to the loss of a contract with a school district in Texas.

IBM's record plunge, Goldman Sachs warns: AI capital expenditures turn to a "software bear market" IBM Corp (https://t.co/v8UbiHOhiQ) issued a surprising second-quarter performance warning on Tuesday, and its stock price hit its largest one-day drop on record.

This triggered deep market concerns about the large-scale shift of enterprise IT budgets to AI infrastructure and the squeeze on traditional software and service spending.

Goldman Sachs directly stated in its first research report that the IBM incident "will fully confirm the software bear market scenario" and predicted that there will be "quite widespread" downward pressure on software and services.

Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider also pointed out that the weakness in IBM's Data & Automation software business is partly due to the company's internal execution problems, and said that he will not adjust the valuation model until next week's earnings call and management provides further explanations on 2026 guidance and response measures.

Wind Financial Morning Post: July 15, 2026

Market Brief

China's first-half economic data, including GDP and credit figures, will be released today.

China's first-half foreign trade volume exceeded 25 trillion yuan, with AI computing power as the strongest growth driver.

Changxin Technology's IPO offering price is set at 8.66 yuan per share, with subscription to open on July 16.
U.S. forces launched a new strike against Iran and have resumed maritime blockade.

Trump reverses stance on Hormuz Strait shipping fees, proposing trade and investment deals instead of a 20% surcharge.

Kevin Warsh's debut congressional hearing: zero tolerance for persistent inflation, Fed launches "blank slate" policy review.

U.S. June CPI inflation eased, marking the first monthly decline in six years.

New York State suspends new large data center construction for one year, the first such ban in the U.S.
Global equities rose Tuesday, with memory chip stocks surging; IBM, after an earnings warning, slid over 25%.
Warren Buffett accelerates charitable giving, planning to liquidate all Berkshire Hathaway shares within eight years.

Top Stories

China's first-half economic data will be released today. The National Bureau of Statistics will announce key indicators including GDP, investment, consumption, and industrial production at 10:00 a.m. The June housing price data for 70 major cities will be released at 9:30 a.m. The State Council Information Office will hold a press conference at 10:00 a.m. with Deputy Director of the National Bureau of Statistics, Mao Shengyong to present the first-half economic performance and answer questions. The State Council Information Office will also hold a briefing at 3:00 p.m. on the first-half monetary policy implementation and financial statistics.

Changxin Technology's STAR Market IPO offering price has been set at 8.66 yuan per share, with subscription to commence on July 16. The offering is expected to raise nearly 58 billion yuan (before exercise of the over-allotment option), making it the largest IPO on the STAR Market to date. If the over-allotment option is fully exercised, total proceeds could reach approximately 66.6 billion yuan. At this price, Changxin Technology's market capitalization at listing is estimated at about 579.2 billion yuan. Strategic investors include major state-backed investment funds, insurance institutions, semiconductor industry players, and downstream application companies. 

A-share listed firms including Montage Technology, Tongfu Microelectronics, and AMEC have each been allocated 18.24 million shares, worth 158 million yuan each.

U.S. forces continued strikes against multiple sites in Iran on July 14, while Iran retaliated against U.S. military bases. The struggle for control over the Strait of Hormuz persists. U.S. President Trump stated that the U.S. will replace the proposed 20% fee on Hormuz Strait shipping with trade and investment agreements with Gulf nations. The U.S. will impose a "complete blockade" on Iran, allowing all vessels except those from Iran to transit the Strait. Iran's military has vowed not to yield on the issue. Iran's parliament has proposed legislation on Strait security.
The U.S. Central Command announced that, at 4:00 p.m. Eastern Time on July 14, it resumed maritime blockade targeting vessels entering or exiting Iranian ports and coastal areas. More than 20 U.S. Navy ships and hundreds of military aircraft are currently deployed in the Middle East.

U.S. inflation rose less than expected in June. Data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics showed that the U.S. June CPI increased 3.5% year-over-year, and core CPI rose 2.6%, both notably lower than the previous reading and below market forecasts. The U.S. June CPI fell 0.4% month-over-month—the first monthly decline in six years and far exceeding the expected 0.1% drop. After the data release, traders pushed back their expectations for a Fed rate hike to October.

Kevin Warsh, in his first semiannual congressional hearing as Fed chair, adopted a hawkish stance, declaring "zero tolerance" for five years of persistent inflation and rejecting market views that inflation is "under control" due to the June CPI decline. Warsh announced the formation of five working groups to conduct a "blank slate" review of the Fed's current policy framework. In response to pressure from the Trump administration for rate cuts, Warsh refused to provide forward guidance, emphasizing that the Fed will base decisions solely on economic data.

China Macro & Policy

According to China Customs, China's total merchandise trade reached 25.47 trillion yuan in the first half of the year, up 16.9% year-over-year—the first time the figure has surpassed 25 trillion yuan in the same period. Electronics components and computer parts—key AI hardware—accounted for 5.13 trillion yuan in trade, up 56.6%. In June alone, China's imports and exports totaled 4.78 trillion yuan, up 24.2%, marking the 17th consecutive month of growth. Rare earth exports surged 178.82% year-over-year.

Wind data shows that, in yuan terms, China's June exports rose 20.8% year-over-year (previous: 13.8%), while imports rose 29.4% (previous: 21.5%). The trade surplus widened to 859.05 billion yuan (previous: 723.98 billion yuan). In U.S. dollar terms, June exports rose 27% (previous: 19.4%), imports rose 36% (previous: 27.4%), and the trade surplus reached $125.62 billion (previous: $105.43 billion).

The People's Bank of China will conduct a 1.4 trillion yuan six-month outright reverse repurchase operation on July 15, the largest single operation since the tool's introduction. Given that 900 billion yuan of this tenor matures in the same month, the operation will result in a net injection of 500 billion yuan—the first time since February that the PBOC has increased the volume of this tenor.

Six ministries released the "15th Five-Year Plan for Solid Waste Pollution Prevention and Control," aiming for significant progress in key sectors by 2030 and full digital monitoring coverage across priority areas. This is China's first five-year special plan targeting solid waste pollution.

The Ministry of Ecology and Environment and other departments issued the "Action Plan for Water Body Protection and Governance Near Communities," targeting issues such as discolored water, foul odors, and floating debris. The goal is to eliminate black and odorous water bodies in county and township areas by 2030.

China Equities Markets

On Tuesday, A-shares staged a sharp rebound in the afternoon, closing with a V-shaped recovery. The SSE Index rose 1.36% to 3,967.13, the Shenzhen Component Index gained 2.77%, the ChiNext Index jumped 3.43%, and the Wind All China Index advanced 1.92%. Market turnover reached 2.72 trillion yuan. The AI computing hardware supply chain surged, with PCB stocks hitting multiple limit-up closures. Telecommunications equipment, precious metals, nonferrous metals, coal, and oil stocks also rose. Commercial aerospace stocks continued to decline.

Hong Kong markets ended slightly higher. The Hang Seng Index rose 0.52% to 24,340.73, the Hang Seng Tech Index gained 0.06%, and the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index rose 0.46%. Nonferrous metals, hardware equipment, semiconductors, oil, and automotive stocks led gains. Southbound funds net bought approximately HK$11 billion, with significant additions to ZhiPu, NetEase, and Kuaishou.

New capital flooded into A-shares, with the total open interest in the CSI 1000 Index Futures (IM) contract surpassing 500,000 contracts for the first time at 500,500—setting a new record. The influx of long positions has broken the market's conventional belief that high open interest equals deep discounting, suggesting potential index recovery.

As of July 13, more than 580 foreign institutions conducted over 3,900 research visits to A-share companies this year. Electronic equipment, semiconductor equipment, and integrated circuits remain the top focus areas. Standard Chartered Bank and Goldman Sachs have issued overweight ratings on A-shares. Morgan Stanley and UBS express optimism on China's equity outlook. Chinese assets remain underweighted in global actively managed portfolios, indicating significant room for future allocation increases.

Sources indicate that DeepSeek has initiated IPO preparations and plans to submit its application by the end of this year or early next year. DeepSeek is also in preliminary discussions with new investors for a funding round, with a pre-money valuation of approximately $71 billion.

Reports suggest that SHEIN will hold its IPO hearing at the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on Thursday, aiming for a listing as early as August. SHEIN seeks to raise $2 billion to $3 billion through the offering.

China Industry News

Recent online posts from state-owned banks promote high-yield deposits claiming interest rates above 5% for large deposits. ICBC, ABC, BOC, and CCB jointly issued a risk warning to debunk the claims, stating these are fraudulent promotions for offshore insurance or asset management products.

China's National Medical Security Administration released the list of drugs passing the preliminary review for the 2026 basic medical insurance and commercial insurance drug catalog adjustments. A total of 601 drugs passed for the basic insurance list and 58 for the commercial insurance list. Passing preliminary review only grants eligibility for expert evaluation, not inclusion in the catalog.

The Ministry of Public Security reported that, as of the end of June, China's total vehicle stock reached 371 million units, of which 13.19% were new energy vehicles. In the first half of the year, 5.195 million new energy vehicles were registered, accounting for 49.42% of all new vehicle registrations.

The National Energy Administration stated that after setting a new record high of 1.518 billion kilowatts on July 10, the national power load reached another record of 1.551 billion kilowatts on July 14. Overall, China's energy supply and demand remain balanced.

Market research firms IDC, Counterpoint, and Omdia reported that global smartphone shipments declined again in the second quarter due to persistent memory shortages, hitting a multi-year low. Samsung, Apple, and Huawei posted counter-cyclical growth, while other major manufacturers saw significant declines.

Fintech

The U.S. and U.K. jointly advanced a transatlantic regulatory framework for stablecoins, releasing policy recommendations to establish clear pathways and eliminate regulatory barriers. The initiative stems from the "Future Markets Transatlantic Working Group" established in September last year, covering cross-border tokenized asset testing, regulatory coordination, and bank capital treatment for crypto-assets.

The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC), the U.S.'s largest securities clearinghouse, will conduct a live blockchain transaction demonstration this week involving equities and U.S. Treasuries. This marks DTCC's first live production test of tokenization processes, with full deployment targeted for October.

Corporate News & Updates

Apple Inc. received a rare downgrade to underweight from KeyBanc Capital Markets, citing concerns over demand and valuation. Apple's forward P/E ratio exceeds 33, significantly above its 10-year average of 23 and the Nasdaq 100's 22.8.

NVIDIA is considering a collaboration with Mitsubishi Heavy Industries on AI data center technology, aiming to integrate Mitsubishi's cooling and energy management systems into NVIDIA's next-generation data centers.

SK Hynix has begun ordering advanced DRAM manufacturing equipment for the first wafer fab in the Yongin semiconductor cluster. Initial production capacity is planned at 20,000 wafers per month, targeting sixth-generation 10nm-class (1c) DRAM.

SoftBank Group founder Masayoshi Son stated that future nuclear fusion technology will provide the most viable solution for AI data center energy demands. Son again dismissed "AI bubble" concerns, predicting that AI development will require $5 trillion in annual investment by 2040, with global data center capacity reaching 3 terawatts.

Just in: Amazing conclusion! JPMorgan: The most pressing issue in the energy market is no longer Hormuz, but Russia.

It is undeniable that as the United States and Iran are once again at war with each other, the situation in the Strait of Hormuz is still the focus of global market attention.

However, as the crack spread, which reflects the difference between the price of crude oil and the price of its refined products, has widened unprecedentedly in recent weeks, some industry professionals are worried that a deeper problem in the energy market may be being overlooked by investors...

As Natasha Kaneva, head of commodity research at JPMorgan Chase, pointed out in the latest "Crude Oil Market Weekly Report", a new round of mutual attacks between the United States and Iran has caused another sharp drop in tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, basically reversing the hard-won recovery momentum in the past few weeks.

However, after months of frequent disruptions, the market has actually become more relaxed when it comes to crude oil supply and tends to view it as a dynamic issue: Crude oil supplies can evaporate instantly and return just as quickly. However, the uncertainty in the refining and chemical sector is much greater right now.

As Kaneva writes, the question is no longer whether crude supplies can be restored, but how quickly the global refining system can process them.

In this regard, there are three main uncertainties: The extent of damage to the Middle East's refining infrastructure after months of conflict; the time it will take for China's refining capacity to be restored; and the speed of Russia's refining system to recover from repeated Ukrainian drone attacks.

On the Middle East front, JPMorgan has tracked more than 30 attacks on various refineries and oil processing infrastructure in the region, but the extent of the actual physical damage remains largely unknown.

The key question is what proportion of the region's 11.7 million barrels per day of refining capacity can be restarted immediately, and what proportion requires extensive maintenance.

In this regard, JPMorgan's baseline forecast is that by the end of this year, only 250,000 barrels per day of production capacity will remain closed, although the bank's confidence in this forecast is relatively low. At the same time, JPMorgan Chase believes that the most underestimated source of uncertainty currently is Russia.

Russia's refining capacity plummets Russia's refining system has been enduring repeated Ukrainian drone attacks over the past three months, which have damaged refineries, storage facilities and, increasingly, the complex secondary conversion units that determine product yields.

Russia's refinery throughput has fallen to 3.8 million barrels per day in June, 1.5 million barrels per day below the level at the beginning of the year, making Russia one of the biggest reasons for the unusually weak global refining activity today.

Data show that global oil refining and processing volume decreased by 8.2 million barrels per day this year, of which Russia alone accounted for about 20%. Domestically, Russia’s fuel supply situation has deteriorated significantly and is increasingly becoming an operational issue, not just a consumer one.

Shortages have spread even to regions with their own refineries as supplies are redirected to Moscow. This reflects a clear shift in targeting in Ukraine: in 2026, refineries supplying oil to Moscow accounted for 39% of all targets, compared with only 22% in 2025.

Fuel shortages are becoming increasingly acute as Russia enters its traditional "dacha" season - when millions of Russians head to their summer villas and seasonal gasoline demand reaches its annual peak. In some areas, drivers waited in line for days, fuel sales were rationed, and gas stations even temporarily ran out of fuel.

Retail prices have risen sharply, with some independent service stations reportedly selling 50% or more above normal levels. The impact of these supply disruptions now extends far beyond private car owners.

Agriculture, public transportation, utilities, logistics and small businesses are being increasingly impacted, signaling a shift from consumer inconvenience to widespread operational disruption across the economy.

Diesel supply issues are even more concerning because agricultural harvesting in Russia's southern regions typically begins in August, when demand for seasonal distillates typically reaches its peak of the year.

In the face of the crisis, the Russian government's current response logic is more focused on ensuring short-term fuel supply security than on substantive repairs of refining capacity.

At present, Russia has begun emergency imports of gasoline from Belarus and India, approved tax amendments aimed at stimulating gasoline production by increasing blending components, and specially approved refineries to produce and sell lower-standard Euro-3 gasoline to replace Euro-5, thereby increasing supply through low-standard blending materials.

As refinery operating rates decline and seasonal demand peaks resonate, Russia has also comprehensively tightened export controls on gasoline, aviation kerosene and diesel.

Xiaomo pointed out that although these measures are pragmatic measures to ensure domestic supply, they also reveal that Russia is currently only able to compensate for fuel shortages at the margin, rather than relying on a fully resilient oil refining system to operate. Will the impact be felt around the world?

JPMorgan predicts the developments could have repercussions far beyond Russia. Unable to process crude oil domestically, Russia will divert more crude oil to the export market, pushing crude oil exports closer to historical highs and exacerbating an already oversupplied crude oil market.

At the same time, the country's refined oil exports will decline sharply. The most affected are middle distillates. Russia's diesel and fuel oil exports fell by about two-thirds, further exacerbating a supply-demand imbalance in an already tight global distillate market and keeping refining margins high.

The reason why this is important is that Russia plays an extremely significant role in the global refined oil trade - it is the world's second largest diesel exporter, supplying approximately 12% of global diesel exports; it is also the largest exporter of fuel oil, with a market share of approximately 16%.

Therefore, disruptions in the Russian refining industry tend to have a far greater impact on the supply and demand balance of refined oil products than on the supply and demand balance of crude oil.

The key question is whether Russia's refining system will eventually return to something close to normal operations or whether it will slip into a less structural equilibrium punctuated by outages.

Before the escalation of drone attacks in early April this year, the normal processing capacity of Russian refineries remained at around 5.3 million barrels per day.

Based on this processing capacity, the system can produce approximately 2 million barrels of diesel, 1 million barrels of gasoline, 900,000 barrels of fuel oil, 300,000 barrels of aviation kerosene and 1.1 million barrels of other refined oil products per day.

Among them, the Russian domestic market consumes most of the gasoline and jet fuel, while nearly half of the diesel and almost all the fuel oil are exported, making it one of the core supply sources of the global distillate market.

Xiaomo pointed out that this normalized operating benchmark is the most effective reference for assessing the depth of current supply disruptions. Currently, the biggest risk to the market is the "persistence" of this shock.

Since late March, Ukraine’s offensive has evolved from sporadic “nuisance attacks” into a premeditated, long-term strategic operation aimed at paralyzing Russia’s entire refining and distribution system.

The frequent attacks, deeper domestic penetration, and wider attack scope all imply that refinery shutdowns are gradually becoming a structural norm in the market, rather than a fleeting temporary disturbance. In addition, Ukraine clearly knows how to hit Russia's "soft underbelly".

Recent attacks are increasingly focused on hydrocracking units, catalytic cracking units, reforming units and other secondary conversion units, and high-frequency "second strikes" are carried out on damaged facilities.

Compared with repairing relatively simple atmospheric distillation units, rebuilding these precision devices is extremely difficult and may directly extend the downtime period from just a few weeks to several quarters. As a result, the refined oil market is tighter than the crude oil market and is very likely to continue until 2027.

JPMorgan's baseline forecast favors a gradual recovery rather than a dramatic and rapid normalization. The bank predicts that the operating rate of Russian refineries will gradually rise from the current 3.6 million barrels per day to about 4.5 million barrels per day in early 2027, with the annual average in 2027 being about 4.7 million barrels per day.

The premise of this prediction is that the intensity of drone attacks will slow down as Ukraine shifts its defense focus back to its own energy infrastructure before the end of Russia's State Duma elections in September and the arrival of the winter heating season.

Even under this scenario, upstream crude oil production is expected to rebound only slightly, as downstream bottlenecks are increasingly affecting upstream investment decisions.

Russia's crude oil production currently remains at 8.7 million barrels per day, a decrease of nearly 600,000 barrels per day from the beginning of the year, which is approaching the actual delivery limit of its crude oil export infrastructure. If refinery utilization cannot be effectively boosted, excess crude oil will have nowhere to put it.

In other words, even though the global market is still naturally focused on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the most critical players in determining the global oil refining balance may have quietly moved to Russia, nearly 2,000 miles north of Hormuz. (

"Zero tolerance" for 5 years of high inflation – "This committee will not tolerate persistently elevated inflation"

Rejected "mission accomplished" – "Some see today's data and declare victory – I do not share that view"

Defended Fed independence – declined forward guidance, emphasized data-dependent decision-making

Launched major reforms – 5 task forces reviewing communication, balance sheet, data, inflation framework

AI as an inflation risk – AI investment is pushing up certain prices, adding a new dimension to inflation pressures

💬Warsh's debut sent one clear signal – the Fed won't be held hostage by one CPI print. Markets wanted a "rate pause" confirmation – they got "inflation surge will eventually become history" instead. He testifies before the Senate Banking Committee Wednesday – every word will be scrutinized.

USD/JPY at 162.2190, -0.15%.

BTC at ~$63,700**, holding steady. **ETH at ~$1,800.

美股三大指数全线收涨——道指微涨0.02%报52508.66点，标普500涨0.38%报7543.89点，纳指涨0.9%报26107.01点。道指大幅低开452点后迅速转升。

芯片股全面反攻：SK海力士ADR暴涨27.2%，美光科技涨近5%，英伟达涨超4%。银行股表现强劲：高盛Q2盈利增84%、收入创新高，股价大涨9%；摩根大通Q2多赚41%，美国银行多赚27%。

但IBM创下历史级暴跌：Q2初步业绩收入及每股盈利均令市场失望，股价急泻25%，录得至少58年以来最大单日跌幅，市值单日蒸发690亿美元。

大型科技股涨跌不一：谷歌涨近2%，Meta、特斯拉、亚马逊小幅收涨；SpaceX跌超2%，微软跌超1%，苹果跌0.77%。

💬 CPI降温给了市场“美联储暂停加息”的理由，芯片股和银行股借此反弹。但IBM的暴跌提醒我们——不是所有公司都在AI浪潮中受益。沃什的鹰派言论则为市场埋下伏笔：别把这份CPI当成“任务完成”的信号。

2. 🏛️ CPI：六年来首次环比负增长，7月加息概率骤降至17%

美国6月CPI数据全面低于预期——整体CPI同比涨3.5%（预期3.8%，前值4.2%），环比下滑0.4%（预期-0.1%），为六年来首次环比负增长。核心CPI同比升2.6%（预期2.8%，前值2.9%），环比录得0%。

能源价格是主要推手——6月能源指数环比下降5.7%，为2020年4月以来最大单月降幅，汽油价格指数环比下降9.7%。

数据公布后，7月加息概率从42%骤降至17%。两年期美债收益率一度下跌14个基点，10年期美债收益率收报4.591%，跌2.61个基点。

💬 这是一份足以改变美联储政策路径的通胀报告。但沃什在听证会上明确警告：“有些人看到今早的数据，就宣称任务完成、万事大吉。我绝不认同这种看法。”油价一周暴涨22%的效应尚未反映在6月数据中——7月的通胀才是真正的考验。

3. 🏛️ 沃什国会首秀：对高通胀“零容忍”，驳斥“任务完成论”

周二，美联储主席沃什首次以主席身份出席众议院金融服务委员会听证会。他在长达三小时的听证中展现出强硬的“鹰派”立场。

核心要点：

对持续五年的高通胀“零容忍” ——“委员会全体成员绝不容忍通胀长期居高不下”

驳斥CPI数据后的“任务完成论” ——“有些人看到今早的数据，就宣称任务完成、万事大吉。我绝不认同”

捍卫美联储独立性——面对特朗普降息施压，拒绝提供前瞻指引，强调依据经济数据独立决策

启动“白纸”大改革——设立五大专项工作组，全面复盘央行沟通机制、资产负债表、经济数据、通胀框架等

关注AI对通胀的影响——AI投资正在推高部分物价，从新维度带来通胀压力

💬 沃什的首次国会亮相传递了一个清晰信号——美联储不会被一份CPI数据绑架。市场想听的“加息暂停”没有得到确认，反而得到了“通胀飙升终将成为历史”的承诺。周三他还将出席参议院银行委员会听证会——市场将继续紧盯他的一字一句。

4. 💵 美债与美元：收益率全线下跌，美元走弱

美债收益率集体下跌——2年期跌7.76个基点报4.193%，10年期跌2.61个基点报4.591%。

美元指数跌0.37%报100.93，因通胀降温削弱了加息预期。欧元兑美元涨0.34%报1.1419，英镑兑美元涨0.33%报1.3390。

美元兑日元报162.2190，跌0.15%。日本财务大臣提出将政府债券纳入个人免税投资计划的想法。

💬 CPI数据是美债和美元的“短期解药”——加息预期骤降→收益率下跌→美元走弱。但沃什的鹰派言论为这份“解药”加上了保质期。4.59%的10年期收益率仍处于历史高位，市场并未完全放松对加息的警惕。

5. 🛢️ 油价：布伦特逼近85美元，特朗普“朝令夕改”

WTI原油收涨1.5%至79.34美元/桶，布伦特原油收涨1.7%至84.73美元/桶，盘中一度涨超4%。

特朗普的“朝令夕改”成为焦点——周一他宣布对通过霍尔木兹海峡的货运征收20%费用，周二在海湾盟友敦促下放弃该计划，改为通过海湾国家对美贸易投资合作替代。但他同时表示将对伊朗实施“全面封锁” ，霍尔木兹海峡对除伊朗外的所有船只开放。

与此同时，中东冲突持续升级——美军继续对伊朗多地发动打击，伊朗向科威特和巴林发射导弹和无人机，阿联酋两艘油轮遭袭造成1死8伤。

💬 特朗普的“保护费”计划只存活了24小时——这本身就是一个市场信号：海湾国家不愿为霍尔木兹海峡的通行买单。但“朝令夕改”并未改变油价的上涨方向——美伊军事对峙仍在持续，霍尔木兹海峡的实际通航状况才是油价的真正定价锚。道明证券策略师警告：“我们需要迅速缓和局势，否则能源市场对这场战争第二轮的反应可能会严重得多”。

6. 🥇 黄金：CPI降温推动反弹，但仍受制于强美元

现货黄金大幅反弹，最终收涨1.27%，亚盘报约4046美元/盎司。现货白银收涨1.79%，亚盘报约58.66美元/盎司。

💬 黄金终于从4000美元下方爬了回来。CPI降温→加息预期骤降→美元走弱→黄金反弹，逻辑链清晰。但4046美元距前期高点仍有距离。在油价高烧、地缘冲突持续、沃什鹰派表态的三重压力下，黄金的反弹能走多远仍是未知数。

7. ₿ 比特币：站稳63000美元，轧空行情延续

比特币报约63,700美元，从昨日低点稳健反弹。以太坊报约1,800美元。

💬 BTC从58K反弹至63.7K——三周反弹近10%。CPI降温→加息预期骤降→流动性预期改善→加密货币反弹，逻辑链清晰。但64K-65K仍是强阻力区间——突破需要更多催化剂。在美伊冲突持续的背景下，加密货币作为风险资产仍面临不确定性。

🔥 整体判断

超级星期二的三重考验尘埃落定，市场给出的答案是：涨，但不坚决。

CPI降温给了市场“美联储暂停加息”的理由——7月加息概率从42%骤降至17%。沃什鹰派又把这个理由打了折扣——“任务远未完成”。油价高烧则提醒市场——中东的炮火不会因为一份CPI数据就熄灭。

三个核心矛盾正在主导市场：

CPI降温 vs 沃什鹰派：数据说“通胀在降”，主席说“别高兴太早”——市场夹在中间

油价高烧 vs 加息预期降温：油价一周涨22%→通胀反弹担忧→但加息预期反而下降——这个矛盾不可持续

地缘冲突 vs 风险偏好：霍尔木兹海峡持续动荡，但美股仍在涨——市场在选择性忽略风险

沃什出席参议院银行委员会听证会——连续第二天，市场继续紧盯每一句话

美伊军事冲突后续——特朗普“全面封锁”伊朗，伊朗誓言“绝不让步”

Tuesday’s low-liquidity session locked in a classic 'stop-run' volatility trap. The thinned matching volume of the mid-week session triggered a mechanical counter-squeeze, temporarily stalling the structural breakdown that tore through Monday's close.

Mainstream financial journalism asserts that the overnight recovery across technology, crude, and industrial commodities was a direct, fundamental response to escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities and the sudden closure of the Strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint. But if you look behind the scenes at our multi-duration force balance matrix, the illusion completely vaporizes: the media is treating a thinned, algorithmic stop-run designed to clean out early short leverage as a brand-new, structural bull expansion.

If you look instead at how the open-source betting networks and decentralized forecasting pools are actively re-pricing these volatile flows, the true map of capital becomes highly transparent:

1. The Regulated Event-Contract Book
A scan of the macroeconomic boards on Kalshi highlights how quickly the crowd is pricing in systemic, long-term inflation friction. Following the overnight escalation in the Gulf, the binary contract tracking whether average U.S. gasoline prices will sit above $3.50 per gallon on Election Day was bid up to a stark 75% probability. While retail commentators talk up a short-lived geopolitical blip, the regulated event-market is acting on structural reality: our matrix shows that the domestic energy deck ( $NATGASUSD) is locked in a high-tension Macro Squeeze phase, pointing to persistent, tight liquidity pipelines. The contract pricing is definitive - smart capital is positioning for a prolonged consumer squeeze that will keep the Fed's hands firmly tied, regardless of near-term paper equity optimism.  For official market-trend data and regulated macro contract tracking, visit Kalshi.  

2. The Peer-to-Peer Betting Pools
Over on the decentralized networks of Polymarket, the intense geopolitical friction triggered a massive re-pricing of global supply chain risk. The contract tracking whether normal shipping traffic will return to the Strait of Hormuz has collapsed to an implied 3% chance of normalising by the end of July. This peer-to-peer capital is tracking raw, physical plumbing data rather than paper sentiment: our core matrix captured Copper ( $XCUUSD) staging a thinned Emerging Bull Breakout, yet its leading 'probable next phase' remains tightly bound to an unyielding Macro Bleed routine. Speculators in the decentralized pools recognize that while paper commodity prices spike on short-term fear, the underlying physical economy is in a state of severe contraction. They are pricing in a prolonged bottleneck that will starve peripheral risk gates of raw liquidity, keeping alternative assets like Bitcoin ( $BTCUSD) heavily exposed to leverage liquidations.

3. The Financial Journalism Narrative

Analysis from any premier editorial desk like is likely as any other to illustrate the superficiality of mainstream market commentary. A column  may claim that Tuesday's overnight surge across Brent Crude ( $BCOUSD) and the tech-heavy Nasdaq ( $NAS100) indicate that the "summer correction is officially over," urging allocators to chase the geopolitical breakout. The EIA has been overestimating US crude inventories by about 13.5 million barrels over the last three weeks. You can see it in the recent spike in the modified adjustment on the chart.

These adjustments often reverse lower later, which means the actual inventory picture could end up tighter than the initial reports suggested. It’s a reminder that weekly EIA numbers can be noisy and subject to revisions.

For the energy side, a tighter actual supply backdrop is generally supportive over time, but these are short-term data points. The bigger picture still comes down to your own process, risk management, and how you weigh the fundamentals versus the weekly noise.

text:
Every Russian missile that hits a Ukrainian apartment block just made an oil tanker in the Black Sea more expendable.

Overnight, Russian strikes killed at least 6–8 people in regions including Odesa and Sumy. In parallel, according to BBC reporting, Ukraine went after around 20 Russian vessels, including oil tankers and other ships supporting the war effort. That is not harassment anymore. It is a strategy: if you hit our cities, we hit the fuel that lets you keep doing it.

For two years the pattern was straightforward: Russia fires from the sea, Ukraine scrambles to shoot everything down. That defensive loop saves lives but does not really change Moscow’s cost curve. Tankers do. They are big, slow, expensive and politically sensitive. Lose a few and you do not just lose fuel – you lose insurance, willing crews, and the illusion that the rear is safe.

Moscow’s mistake was treating logistics ships as background scenery, not targets. If Kyiv turns this into a sustained campaign, Russia either pulls its support vessels back – shrinking its real reach in the Black Sea – or sails them forward under constant threat.

The war at sea is sliding from ships versus missiles to missiles versus supply lines. That is where campaigns are won or slowly bled out.
